title: Cooper Flagg was asked if he wants to be a Celtic one day. Here’s what the Maine native said.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cooper Flagg, a Maine native, plays his first NBA game at TD Garden with the Dallas Mavericks.
Flagg, drafted No. 1 overall by the Mavericks, expresses commitment to the team and enjoys playing in Boston.
Under contract until at least 2028, Flagg averages 20.3 points and 6.5 rebounds in his rookie NBA season.

title: With Jayson Tatum back in the fold, these Celtics know their story is just beginning
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics after 42 weeks, scoring 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists against the Mavericks.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's effort despite shooting struggles, noting it was Tatum's approach and defense that impressed him.
Nikola Vucevic will miss one month due to a broken finger, impacting the Celtics' roster as they enter the playoff season.

title: Jayson Tatum’s return was essentially perfect: 7 takeaways as Celtics blow out Mavericks
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned without a minutes restriction in the Celtics' 120-100 victory over the Mavericks.
Tatum scored 15 points, grabbed 12 rebounds, and made seven assists, showing some rust but also flashes of his talent.
Nikola Vucevic is out for a month due to a fractured finger, and Jaylen Brown led the team with 24 points.

title: Why Bruins made right call to stand pat at trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney chose not to make major trade moves, focusing on the team's long-term rebuilding goals.
Boston traded AHL players for prospects, avoiding costly deals in a competitive trade market.
The Bruins maintain four first-round picks over two years, positioning them for future success with emerging prospects.

title: Patriots reportedly miss out on Maxx Crosby trade
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Maxx Crosby to be traded to the Ravens from the Raiders for two first-round picks, the No. 14 pick and a 2027 pick.
Crosby, who expressed interest in the Patriots, had reportedly considered New England but will not join them.
Patriots with over $60 million cap space intended to strengthen their roster but likely won't match Ravens' offer for Crosby.

title: Report: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic to miss one month with broken finger
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic fractures right ring finger against Dallas, expected to miss one month after surgery.
Vucevic's injury leaves Celtics thin at center with Jayson Tatum returning from Achilles' injury and limited depth options.
Vucevic averaged 11.4 points, 8.8 rebounds since joining team; Celtics face Cavaliers next and will update his status Saturday.

title: Watch: Jayson Tatum receives thunderous ovation in first game back with Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to play for the Boston Celtics after recovering from a ruptured Achilles, drawing a loud ovation at TD Garden.
Tatum played six minutes in his NBA season debut, recording two assists and two rebounds, scoring a put-back dunk and a 3-pointer.
The Celtics, despite Tatum missing 62 games, are in second place in the Eastern Conference with a 41-21 record.

title: Report: Patriots to trade C Garrett Bradbury to Bears
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Patriots will trade center Garrett Bradbury to the Chicago Bears for a fifth-round draft pick.
The trade frees up $5.7 million in cap space for the Patriots, aiding their free agency plans.
Bradbury, 30, started every game in 2025, contributing to the Patriots' AFC Championship-winning season.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Hamnet,' a speculative drama about Shakespeare's personal tragedy, is now streaming on Peacock.
'It Was Just an Accident,' critiquing Iran's regime, is streaming on Hulu, directed by Jafar Panahi.
Netflix is losing rights to 'Arrested Development' on March 15; available for a limited time.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics starting lineup
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returns to Celtics starting lineup for the first time this season against the Mavericks.
Coach Joe Mazzulla commends Tatum's work ethic and openness during his recovery and return process.
Tatum replaces Baylor Scheierman in the lineup with no announced minutes restriction from Mazzulla.
